critic_raw:
The test-time adaptation strategy uses exemplar objects from the same image being evaluated. This is transductive inference — the model adapts using the target instance before predicting on it — which is a fundamentally different evaluation regime from inductive generalization to held-out images. Reporting test-time-adapted results alongside non-adaptive baselines without clearly distinguishing transductive from inductive evaluation inflates the comparison.

FSC-147 was introduced by the FamNet authors and its design choices (exemplar format, annotation style, image curation) may systematically favor FamNet's exemplar-based architecture. Detection-based counting methods require bounding boxes and may be disadvantaged by FSC-147's annotation format. Evaluating on author-created benchmarks without independent benchmark validation is a standard circular evaluation failure.

The comparison against 'category-specific' counting methods uses models trained on specialized single-category datasets (e.g., counting cars, cells). If these specialized models were not fine-tuned on the FSC-147 test categories, the comparison is against out-of-distribution baselines, making FamNet's improvement attributable to domain mismatch rather than superior counting ability.

The 'increasing exemplars reduces error' claim requires specifying a diminishing returns analysis. If accuracy effectively saturates at 2 exemplars, the multi-exemplar mechanism adds architectural complexity with negligible practical benefit, and the evaluation should characterize this plateau explicitly.
all_issues_raised: Test-time adaptation using exemplars from the same target image is transductive inference; comparing it to inductive non-adaptive baselines without clearly distinguishing evaluation regimes inflates FamNet's apparent advantage.

FSC-147 was designed by the FamNet authors, and its annotation format may systematically disadvantage detection-based counting methods; evaluating on an author-created benchmark without independent validation is circular.

Category-specific baselines may be evaluated out-of-distribution relative to FSC-147 test categories; FamNet's improvement over them may reflect domain mismatch rather than superior general counting ability.

The multi-exemplar mechanism's diminishing returns are not characterized; if accuracy saturates at 2 exemplars, the additional complexity adds no practical value and the evaluation should report this plateau.
all_issues_adjudicated: Test-time adaptation using exemplars from the same target image is transductive inference; comparing it to inductive non-adaptive baselines without clearly distinguishing evaluation regimes inflates FamNet's apparent advantage.

FSC-147 was designed by the FamNet authors, and its annotation format may systematically disadvantage detection-based counting methods; evaluating on an author-created benchmark without independent validation is circular.

Category-specific baselines may be evaluated out-of-distribution relative to FSC-147 test categories; FamNet's improvement over them may reflect domain mismatch rather than superior general counting ability.

The multi-exemplar mechanism's diminishing returns are not characterized; if accuracy saturates at 2 exemplars, the additional complexity adds no practical value and the evaluation should report this plateau.
